note: Canonical job-free exact clean active integrated technical regression matrix; it validates DCL combat mechanisms with retired Approach and Fear compatibility controls absent, native Disable/Immobilize counter transfer absent, and preserves no final balance or job-design claims.

cases.purpose: Bind every live observation to the exact active integrated settings, action data, code DLL, Enhanced target, and three-mod Reloaded profile.
cases.fixture: Closed game with the canonical active integrated runtime/data pair selected; no battle state is required.
actions: Run the declared process-free live-install preflight command.
Do not launch FFT unless the command exits zero.
pass_evidence: The validator prints DCL live installation preflight PASS.
The emitted hashes equal the pair-bound settings, action NXD, and current Release DLL hashes.

cases.purpose: Validate one complete physical decision from forecast through execution, including facing, bounded guard, damage typing, equipment metadata, weapon-skill routing, and native presentation.
cases.fixture: Two controllable generic units with known facing and existing equipment; repeat from front and rear without changing job definitions.
actions: Preview and execute a single weapon strike from the front.
Repeat against the same target from the rear while preserving equipment.
Repeat once with a weapon-skill carrier whose active weapon is known.
pass_evidence: Forecast and execution use the same authoritative hit decision and finite guard bounds.
Logs expose facing, damage type, equipment DR, item metadata, and active weapon provenance without changing animation or target presentation.
The execution result equals the committed DCL result and produces no duplicate application.

cases.purpose: Separate dual-wield, managed multistrike, and native repeat provenance so each hit is owned exactly once.
cases.fixture: A dual-wield basic attacker, Pummel ability 101, and Barrage ability 358 against a durable single target.
actions: Execute a dual-wield basic attack and preserve both native weapon identities.
Execute Pummel and record every managed repeat index.
Execute Barrage and record its native repeat index and active weapon payload.
pass_evidence: Dual-wield produces two ordered strikes with the correct weapon for each strike.
Pummel produces one transaction per managed repeat with no carry-over decision.
Barrage remains native-repeat owned and does not receive a second managed repeat layer.

cases.purpose: Validate numeric magic computation, magic evasion, and AI scoring against the same executable outcome.
cases.fixture: Fire ability 16 aimed at one isolated legal target with known Faith and magic evade.
actions: Preview Fire against the isolated target.
Allow an AI-controlled caster to score the same target state.
Execute Fire and capture the final numeric result and hit decision.
pass_evidence: Player forecast, AI score inputs, and execution expose the same DCL numeric compute point.
Magic evade participates once and the execution decision matches the committed result.

cases.purpose: Prove authoritative target expansion and one calculation per legal target for an ordinary area spell without importing any status or forced-control mechanic.
cases.fixture: Fire ability 16 centered so at least two legal units occupy its area while another unit remains outside it.
actions: Confirm Fire with the multi-target area visible.
Capture pre-confirm actor targets, expanded targets, and per-target calculations.
Let the spell resolve through its ordinary per-target calculations.
pass_evidence: The runtime records ability 16 rather than ability 0.
The authoritative/expanded target set contains exactly the units in Fire's area and each resolved target receives one calculation.
No unrelated status or forced-control transaction is attributed to Fire.

cases.purpose: Validate healing as a first-class numeric result and preserve native Undead inversion semantics.
cases.fixture: Cure ability 1 used once on a wounded living target and once on a wounded Undead target with comparable stats.
actions: Preview and execute Cure on the living target.
Preview and execute Cure on the Undead target.
pass_evidence: The living target receives the DCL healing result exactly once.
The Undead target receives the correctly inverted HP transaction without sign ambiguity or double inversion.

cases.purpose: Validate MP affordability and atomic HP/MP/result-flag commit at the execution boundary.
cases.fixture: A caster with enough MP for one Fire cast and a controlled follow-up state near the affordability boundary.
actions: Execute one affordable Fire and capture HP, MP, and result flags before and after commit.
Attempt the same action from a state below its MP cost.
pass_evidence: The affordable action commits one coherent HP/MP/flags transaction.
The unaffordable action cannot create damage, a status packet, or a partial MP debit.
MP trickle does not bypass the action's affordability decision.

cases.purpose: Exercise ordinary, retained-native, post-calc replacement, and grouped status ownership without overlapping writers.
cases.fixture: Kiyomori 81, Masamune 84, and Celestial Stasis 168 against targets with controlled resistances and existing status bits.
actions: Resolve each retained-native carrier and verify its native status bits remain authoritative.
Resolve a suppressed ordinary conditional rider once successfully and once resisted.
Resolve the grouped Celestial Stasis packet and capture the complete add/remove mask.
pass_evidence: Every status bit has one declared writer policy.
Retained bits survive unchanged, post-calc-owned bits are composed once, and grouped rules share one contest decision.
Resisted rules do not leak partial status writes.

cases.purpose: Validate random-one status grouping across performance and RandomFire repeat families without combinatorial packet growth.
cases.fixture: Nameless Song 91 and Celestial Void 173 with multiple legal targets and enough repetitions to expose stale group state.
actions: Execute Nameless Song and record the selected group member for every affected target.
Execute Celestial Void and record every native RandomFire repeat and selected status member.
Advance to another action and confirm prior group state is absent.
pass_evidence: Each target/repeat selects at most one member from its declared contest group.
Performance and RandomFire actions finish with bounded log/packet counts and no progressive slowdown.
No group selection leaks into the next action.

cases.purpose: Validate physical and magical duration ownership against the exact action-data/status-duration pair.
cases.fixture: Leg Shot 213 and Arm Shot 214 for the physical domain; Immobilize 37 and Hesitation 57 for the magical domain.
actions: Apply each physical duration carrier and count target turns until expiry.
Apply each magical duration carrier and count target turns until expiry.
Repeat one resisted application to prove that no timer is created.
pass_evidence: Physical and magical duration formulas produce the pair-declared target-turn count.
Expiry occurs once at the intended target-turn boundary.
A resisted status has neither a bit nor a duration counter.

cases.purpose: Validate forced-target fallback and pending-action cancellation as distinct control mechanisms.
cases.fixture: Berserk ability 241 as the Taunt carrier and Potion action 368 as the temporary Interrupt carrier against a unit with a known pending action.
actions: Apply the Taunt carrier and observe target selection with and without a legal taunt target.
Apply the Interrupt carrier to a matching pending action once resisted and once successful.
Repeat after the pending action has already cleared.
pass_evidence: Taunt constrains only an eligible action and falls back safely when its target is unavailable.
Successful Interrupt clears exactly one matching pending action; resistance and no-pending cases perform no write.
Neither mechanism corrupts the other mechanism's state.

cases.purpose: Validate DCL instant-KO ownership and the native corpse/revive lifecycle as one coherent defeat boundary.
cases.fixture: Death ability 30 against success and resistance targets, followed by Raise ability 5 on the resulting corpse.
actions: Resolve Death on the success target and capture the zero-HP/KO transition.
Resolve Death on the resistance target.
Use Raise on the corpse created by the successful case.
pass_evidence: Successful Death commits one KO transition and failure commits neither damage nor KO.
The corpse remains a valid native revive target.
Raise restores the unit through the native lifecycle without duplicate death or stale DCL state.

cases.purpose: Validate real, VM-internal, and synthetic reactions through their distinct ownership paths.
cases.fixture: Counter 442, unknown blank owner carrier 443, and Shirahadori 451 in deterministic adjacent/non-adjacent hit fixtures.
actions: Trigger a real Counter and capture native materialization/delivery.
Trigger Shirahadori and capture VM-internal decision evidence without expecting a queue fire.
Trigger the synthetic owner/delivery route and capture reservation, validation, and execution.
pass_evidence: Counter is classified and delivered once by the native path.
Shirahadori is classified by its VM-internal outcome and is not falsely required to materialize as a normal reaction record.
Synthetic delivery uses the declared owner/carrier split and executes exactly once after validation.

cases.purpose: Validate exactly one post-movement event per completed route and no event for preview, cancellation, Wait, or current-tile selection.
cases.fixture: One player-controlled mover plus at least one AI mover in an ordinary battle; no authored final-tile effect is enabled.
actions: Let one AI unit complete a nonempty route.
Confirm one nonempty player route and wait for its walking animation to finish.
On a later player turn preview a different tile, cancel Move, and complete the turn with Wait.
Exercise Move against the unit's current tile without displacement.
pass_evidence: Each completed AI/player route publishes exactly one accepted completed-movement row after coordinate convergence.
Preview/cancel, Wait without movement, and current-tile selection publish no player event.
Movement remains smooth and indivisible, with no hook failure, per-tile event, pause, slowdown, or game-state write.

cases.purpose: Preserve weapon line-of-fire constraints and expose weight-dependent movement without assigning either rule to a final job.
cases.fixture: A ranged weapon user with a blocking unit/terrain variant and two otherwise identical units carrying different existing equipment weights.
actions: Preview and execute the ranged attack with a clear line and with an obstruction.
Move both weighted units along the same legal path and capture route cost/final tile.
pass_evidence: Clear and obstructed weapon lines produce the expected legal/illegal targeting result without bypassing native presentation.
Weight changes only the declared movement budget/cost and neither unit commits an illegal tile.

cases.purpose: Confirm that native special-action families outside normal formula ownership still execute and are classified without a destructive rewrite.
cases.fixture: One representative native special action from each catalogued non-standard execution family available in the test roster.
actions: Preview and execute every selected native-special representative.
Capture classification, targeting, and final result for each family.
pass_evidence: Each special action remains on its native execution route unless the clean-v1 pair explicitly declares an owner.
No action is silently coerced into ordinary physical or magic semantics and no result is duplicated.

cases.purpose: Prove all transient DCL ownership state is generation-scoped and cannot leak across actions, battle end, or a new battle.
cases.fixture: A battle that has exercised status groups, hit decisions, Interrupt, synthetic Reaction, and final-tile publications before ending; then start a fresh battle without saving.
actions: End the populated battle after all listed mechanisms have created transient state.
Start a fresh battle in the same process.
Execute one ordinary action and inspect every cache/generation diagnostic.
pass_evidence: Battle end and battle start each emit the expected generation reset.
The new battle contains no stale target, status plan, hit decision, pending cancellation, Reaction reservation, or final-tile publication state.
The first ordinary action behaves identically to a clean-process baseline.
